title: Why did Asuka think Shinji got fired
display_title: Why did Asuka think Shinji got fired
wikitext:
[]: Why did Asuka think Shinji got fired?             Quote Post by angelofgendo » Thu Oct 12, 2023 12:52 am When Shinji discovers that Asuka has moved in with him and Misato in episode 9, she tells him he got fired that same day. She even says "didn't you get the memo?" Then, Misato carries on and goes right into saying the two of them need to work together to defeat the angel. Was Asuka lying to try to get him to leave so he would be permanently out of her way, or did she think their previous failure with the last angel was so obviously his fault that of course he'd be let go? What do you guys think?

[]: Re: Why did Asuka think Shinji got fired?             Quote Post by RussianRiz » Thu Oct 12, 2023 9:47 am She was probably inflating the situation to seem like she was given some sort of privilege. I don't think she actually believed he was thrown out.

[]: Re: Why did Asuka think Shinji got fired?             Quote Post by thewayneiac » Thu Oct 12, 2023 10:54 am ^Or Misato felt that Asuka would refuse to come if she knew Shinji would still be there, so She let her think she was replacing Shinji until after she was moved in.

[]: Re: Why did Asuka think Shinji got fired?             Quote Post by angelofgendo » Thu Oct 12, 2023 6:34 pm RussianRiz wrote: She was probably inflating the situation to seem like she was given some sort of privilege. I don't think she actually believed he was thrown out.

[]: Re: Why did Asuka think Shinji got fired?             Quote Post by IronEvangelion » Wed Oct 18, 2023 10:31 am I think that, due to her ego, when she got to the apartment she saw Shinji's stuff and just assumed that her moving in meant he had been fired. In her mind, there was no way Misato could expect her to live together with someone she regarded as inferior.

Was Asuka lying to try to get him to leave so he would be permanently out of her way, or did she think their previous failure with the last angel was so obviously his fault that of course he'd be let go? What do you guys think?
